mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the threshold crossing shows no predictive power for forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in retail sales indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, rising consumer price inflation reduces real disposable income and dampens consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%, or the conditional correlation between inflation and equity returns flips sign during high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth continues for >6 consecutive months while SPX 20d forward returns are negative in >50% of those months.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.2 million annualized units, it signals a sharp deceleration in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite HOUST dropping below 1200 for two consecutive months, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a policy-induced economic slowdown that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint rise in UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong GDP growth, a decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks that compress margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO falls while GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the conditional predictive power disappears in a 5-year out-of-sample test.
